definitions.text: From one side or end of (something) to the other. So as to enter (something), pass within or across, and then leave.
examples: I drove through Lausanne on my way from Geneva to Zurich.
The bullet went right through his leg.

definitions.text: So as to enter (something), pass within or across, and then leave.
examples: I drove through Lausanne on my way from Geneva to Zurich.
The bullet went right through his leg.

definitions.text: So as to progress within (something) or towards the end or limit of (something).
examples: The virus broke out in the capital city and is now spreading quickly through the country.
I'm working through this box set of 'Downton Abbey'.

definitions.text: Passing from one side of something to the other.
examples: Interstate highways form a nationwide system of through roads.
They were through with laying the subroof by noon.

definitions.text: Along the course of a task etc.; used in expressions of progress towards the end.
examples: It's a big job but we're halfway through.

definitions.text: From one side of something to the other. By way of the interior.
examples: I didn't like the look of the place, so I drove through without stopping.
I didn't like the look of the place, so I drove through without stopping.
I opened the window and climbed through.

definitions.text: A coffin, sarcophagus or tomb of stone; a large slab of stone laid on a tomb, or in a dry-stone wall from one side to the other; a perpend.
